meaning: n.鐤剧梾(灏ゆ寚寰仚),涓嶅畞,涓嶅畨
examples: No ailment was found, and he investigated again.
Asthma, a bronchial condition, is much less common ailment than hay fever, an allergic inflammation of the nasal passages.
If identical twins are more similar to each other with respect to an ailment than fraternal twins are, then vulnerability to the disease must be rooted at least in part in heredity.
Mrs Arroyo, who suffers from a spine-related ailment, is in hospital for dehydration, reports say.
Her unemployed in-laws, one of whom has a serious heart ailment, had to move in.
